when_to_use: late-night, intimate, a doubt->acceptance arc, 68-80 BPM, Rhodes-led

defaults.feel: relaxed swung-16th, snare and keys a touch behind the beat

signature_moves: chromatic slide: Dm9 -> Db7#11 -> Cm9 -> B7#11
static-bass reharm: hold F in the bass, move Fmaj9 -> Fm(maj9) -> Ebmaj9
cadence_options: tritone sub turnaround: Gm11 -> Gb13 -> Fmaj9
avoid: plain ii-V-I with no reharm or extension (see ../references/cliche-register.md)
the same voicing block copy-pasted every bar

hook_archetypes.name: rise-and-settle
hook_archetypes.rhythm: syncopated 16th pickup into a held target note across the barline
hook_archetypes.contour: rise a 6th from the 5th, then settle down to the 3rd

hook_archetypes.name: call-and-answer
hook_archetypes.rhythm: two short answering phrases, the second landing a beat later than the first
hook_archetypes.contour: a falling 4th answered by a rising 2nd that lands on the 9th

doubt.note_lengths: short phrases with long gaps between them
doubt.contour: gentle rise then fall, stopping on the 6th (unresolved)

hope.contour: climb, each phrase reaching a little higher
hope.placement: closer to on the beat as energy tightens

peak.note_lengths: hold the peak note; let it ring
peak.contour: sustain the high target tone over open harmony

acceptance.placement: loosen, drift behind the beat again

drum_skeleton.low_energy: kick on 1 and the '&' of 3, snare laid-back on 2 and 4, hi-hat sparse quarter notes
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: add off-beat hi-hat 16ths as ghosts (vel < 45), an occasional kick pickup before beat 1
drum_skeleton.high_energy: busier hi-hat 16ths, snare ghost notes around the backbeat, a fill leading into the peak section

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: keys+bass state the intro alone; drums enter with the first full A; drop drums (or go to brushed/sparse) for the bridge; lead lays out during the keys turnaround so the comp breathes

anti_boredom_rules: the chosen hook must reappear at least twice, always varied (register, rhythm, or harmonization) - never an identical copy
at least one section drops one instrument for contrast
no 2-bar loop repeats more than 4 times without a fill, reharm, or dynamic shift
velocity is never flat across a voice within a bar (ghost notes low, targets/accents high)
use at least one signature_move from the palette somewhere - do not stay purely diatonic for the whole tune
